item.title: Getting started with HugoKit
item.description: Add your first Hugo site, start the local server, and see live reload in action — without touching the terminal.

item.title: Deploying to GitHub Pages
item.description: Connect your GitHub account once, then push every site to Pages with a single click.

item.title: Faster drafting with the Content card
item.description: Draft toggles, front-matter health checks, and quick-new content — how to keep writing without breaking the build.

item.title: The thinking behind HugoKit
item.description: Why a Hugo site deserves a native macOS home — and what I tried to leave out.

item.title: Post 3
item.description: Occaecat aliqua consequat laborum ut ex aute aliqua culpa quis irure esse magna dolore quis. Proident fugiat labore eu laboris officia Lorem enim. item.description:

item.title: Post 1
item.description:

item.title: Command palette
item.description: ⌘K anywhere in the app opens a searchable list of every HugoKit action.

item.title: Configuration
item.description: Visual editor for hugo.toml, hugo.yaml, and hugo.json — with validation and format conversion.

item.title: Content
item.description: Browse, count, and create Markdown content per site — with front-matter health surfaced inline.

item.title: Publish
item.description: Deploy the active site to GitHub Pages or any FTP / SFTP host, with smart sync and preflight checks.

item.title: Server
item.description: Run Hugo’s local preview server directly from HugoKit, with live reload and structured log output.

item.title: Site switcher
item.description: Top-of-sidebar control that flips between every Hugo site you’ve added to HugoKit.
